zh.Thorley, Hertfordshire, England, United: 英國英格蘭賀特福爾利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice De Aston於大約1411年出生在英國斯塔福爾時，她的父親Robert De Aston III23歲，母親Isabel Beeston17歲。她於大約1426年與John Wells在英國斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1469年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾享年59歲。

zh-hans.Thorley, Hertfordshire, England, United: 英国英格兰賀特福爾利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice De Aston于大约1411年出生在英国斯塔福爾时，她的父亲Robert De Aston III23岁，母亲Isabel Beeston17岁。她于大约1426年与John Wells在英国斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1469年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Ad(e)stan, which is usually from Old English Æthelstān (see Alston ). It may also be from the much rarer Old English name Ēadstān.
habitational name from any of several places in England called Aston. Most were named with Old English ēast ‘east’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In a few cases the first element is æsc ‘ash tree’.

variants.name: Alston
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Alstan, which is a coalescence of several different Old English personal names: Æthelstān ‘noble stone’, Ælfstān ‘elf stone’, Ealdstān ‘old stone’, or Ealhstān ‘temple stone’.

habitational name from any of various places called Alston (in Cumbria, Lancashire, Devon, and Somerset) or Alstone (in Gloucestershire and Staffordshire). With the exception of Alston in Cumbria, which is formed with the Old Norse personal name Halfdan, these placenames all consist of an Old English personal name + Old English tūn ‘settlement’, for example Ælfsige in the case of Alstone in Gloucestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice De Aston was born about 1411, in Staffordshire, England, her father, Robert De Aston III, was 23 and her mother, Isabel Beeston, was 17. She married John Wells about 1426, in Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1469, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.
